# Okada / Keke Rider Co-op Platform — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-okada-keke-okada-keke-coop  
**Vertical slug:** okada-keke  
**Niche family:** NF-TRP-MIC (standalone)  
**Family role:** standalone  
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Despite a Lagos State Government ban on okadas (motorcycle taxis) in 2020 on major roads, the sector remains massive in the rest of Nigeria — Kano, Ibadan, Abuja (Gwagwalada, Bwari), Port Harcourt, Benin City, Enugu, and all secondary cities. Keke NAPEP (tricycle) is the urban transport backbone across Nigeria. An estimated 5 million okada and keke riders operate in Nigeria. They are organised into co-operatives and unions (NURTW, RTEAN, Road Transport Employers' Association). A co-op platform enables route registration, rider membership, levy payment, insurance coordination, and WhatsApp dispatch.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
**Persona A — Musa Tanko, 31, Kano**: Okada rider, member of a motorcycle co-op. Wants to check his route allocation, pay his weekly levy, and see insurance status.
**Persona B — Amara Okafor, 27, Ibadan**: Keke driver wanting to join a recognised co-op to get a road permit and avoid police harassment.
**Persona C — Mr Emmanuel Eze, 48, Port Harcourt**: Co-op chairman wanting a portal to manage 500 members — levy records, route maps, dispute resolution.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
**Home:** Hero (co-op name, "Safe. Organised. Profitable." tagline), membership benefits, route listing, registration CTA (WhatsApp), trust strip (NURTW/RTEAN affiliation, state government permit, years in operation).
**About:** Co-op history, leadership, state registration, affiliated unions.
**Services:** Membership process, levies in ₦, insurance scheme, route coverage, safety training.
**Contact:** WhatsApp dispatch/registration, office address, NDPR form.

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Community-first, empowering, worker-solidarity. "Ride together, grow together." Nigerian English. Primary CTA: "Join the Co-op via WhatsApp." Respectful of riders' dignity — avoid condescending language.

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
NURTW/RTEAN affiliation badge; State Ministry of Transport permit number; CAC cooperative registration; years of operation; membership count badge ("Over 500 active members"); weekly levy transparency; insurance partner (LEADWAY, Cornerstone, AIICO); police liaison contact for harassment cases.

## 6. Image Direction
1. Keke NAPEP riders queuing at a bus stop in Kano or Enugu — organised, branded vests.
2. Okada rider in helmet and orange co-op vest waiting for passengers at Ibadan junction.
3. Co-op office — chairman at desk with registration papers, members' cards displayed.
4. WhatsApp dispatch screen — organised route assignments, Nigerian phone.
5. Safety training session — riders in circle being trained on traffic rules.
6. Insurance card being issued to rider by co-op officer.
7. Early morning rider muster — 200 keke operators at a park entrance, sunrise.

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
State Road Traffic Law compliance. FRSC: riders must have valid licences — encourage display of compliance. State Ministry of Transport: co-op registration permit. NURTW/RTEAN: affiliation display. Insurance: mandatory third-party (Motor Third Party Policy per NAICOM). Lagos ban note: template should include location-specific advisory if state bans okada.

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
| Requirement | Status |
|---|---|
| WhatsApp CTA home + contact | ✅ REQUIRED |
| All prices in ₦ (levy, fare) | ✅ REQUIRED |
| Nigerian cities | ✅ Kano, Ibadan, Port Harcourt, Enugu |
| NURTW/RTEAN trust signal | ✅ REQUIRED |
| Paystack / bank transfer | ✅ REQUIRED |
| Mobile-first 375px | ✅ REQUIRED |
| NDPR consent | ✅ REQUIRED |
